description_long: This object is a compact arcade-style joystick controller, featuring a rectangular black base with rounded corners and a metallic gray top panel. Prominently positioned on the left side is a red ball-top joystick, which is used for directional input. To the right of the joystick, there are eight circular buttons arranged in two rows, with six red buttons and two yellow buttons, likely for gameplay actions and additional functions. The top panel is secured with visible screws at the corners, giving it a robust and industrial appearance. The controller is designed to be placed on a flat surface, such as a table or arcade cabinet, and is typically used for video game interaction. Its sturdy build and tactile controls make it suitable for both casual and competitive gaming environments.
description: A robust arcade joystick controller with a red ball-top stick and eight colored buttons on a metallic panel, set in a black rectangular base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the joystick on the left and all eight buttons clearly, with the two yellow buttons on the far right.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the joystick's height and the staggered arrangement of the buttons, with the yellow buttons at the back.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the button layout from behind, with the joystick now on the right and the yellow buttons on the left.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the joystick's profile and the alignment of the buttons, with the yellow buttons at the front.
